nameMeanings.name: sir)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Sier  , unexplained. This surname is very rare in Germany.
Czech (Šír): nickname from Middle High German schīr ‘sheer, pure, clean’. Compare Schier .

variants.name: Sier
meanings: Dutch and German: from a short form of the personal name Sierik, Sierich, composed of the ancient Germanic elements sigi ‘victory’ + rīc ‘power(ful)’, or from a short form of the personal name Sigihard, composed of the ancient Germanic elements sigi + hari ‘army’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) was born about 1280, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. He married Sibil of Northampton Fourneux about 1300, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) est né environ 1280, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Sibil of Northampton Fourneux environ 1300, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) wurde um 1280 in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat um 1300 in England, United Kingdom Sibil of Northampton Fourneux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) è nato il circa 1280 nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Sibil of Northampton Fourneux circa 1300, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.<Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, United: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir)は約1280年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスで生まれた。約1300年にイギリスイギリスでSibil of Northampton Fourneuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir)(은)는 대략 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1300년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sibil of Northampton Fourneux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) nasceu aproximadamente 1280, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Sibil of Northampton Fourneux aproximadamente 1300, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) родился около 1280 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Sibil of Northampton Fourneux около 1300 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Fourneux ( sir) nació aproximadamente 1280, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Sibil of Northampton Fourneux aproximadamente 1300, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
